name: v05-polling-with-backoff
description: v0.6 wait-inside-primitives Phase 3 demo — Loop with wait inside the iteration body. The handler initialises an `attempt` counter, then enters a `loop` that runs while `attempt < 3`. Each iteration bumps the counter and waits 100ms before re-evaluating the condition. The wait inside the iteration body is the load-bearing test of v0.6 Phase 3: each iteration's wait fires `WaitDispatchRequest` from inside the loop iteration scope, the runner persists the iteration cursor to `node_runs.iteration_context`, defers the run, then on `dispatchDeferred` re-entry LoopNode reads the cursor and resumes iteration N at innerStepIndex 1 (the wait), which satisfies because the timer has fired. The while-condition is NOT re-evaluated on the first iteration after resume — the condition was already true when the wait fired and the cursor commits the runner to finishing iteration N's body. Real-world analogue: poll an external job until it's ready with exponential backoff, schedule-then-check loops with bounded retry. Smoke validates the first 202 + Location header (full round-trip is verified in the vitest integration suite).
